executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has reached a critical new phase with first confirmed US aircraft loss to Iranian air defenses (F-15 shot down in Gulf), while Iran reports 555 killed in US-Israeli strikes and explicitly rejects all diplomatic engagement. The conflict now spans eight fronts including direct attacks on US embassy in Kuwait, 5th Fleet base in Bahrain, and Saudi oil facilities. Regional escalation accelerates with Hezbollah formally entering the war and Houthi threats intensifying.
whats_list_new: Iranian air defense forces shot down US Air Force F-15 fighter jet — first confirmed loss of US aircraft to Iranian air defenses
US strike damaged Gandhi Hospital in Tehran — escalation into civilian medical infrastructure
Chinese citizen killed in US strikes on Tehran — first confirmed foreign civilian casualty
Iranian Red Crescent announces 555 killed in Iran from American-Israeli strikes — first official casualty count
IRGC claimed attack on Israeli PM Netanyahu's office and Air Force commander headquarters
confirmed_events: Iran launched tenth wave of ballistic missiles targeting Israeli government and military sites (Tel Aviv, Haifa, Jerusalem) — confirmed by IRGC statement
Iran directly attacked US embassy compound in Kuwait — significant escalation onto sovereign US territory
Iranian missiles struck Salman Port naval base hosting US 5th Fleet in Bahrain — confirmed attack on US naval infrastructure
Hezbollah formally announced entry into war, launching rockets and drones at Israel in retaliation for Khamenei assassination
Qatar deployed Patriots and fighter jets to intercept Iranian missiles and drones protecting Al-Udeid Air Base
UAE condemns Iranian attacks as dangerous escalation, closes Tehran embassy — diplomatic fallout accelerating
unverified_emerging: Kuwait Ministry of Defense announced downing of American warplanes (specific numbers unconfirmed) — unverified whether this refers to F-15 or additional aircraft
Iran claims Natanz nuclear site was hit; IAEA states no evidence of nuclear facilities being struck — unverified, potential major escalation if confirmed
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi states military units now 'independent and isolated' — suggests potential fractures in command structure but unverified

people_travel: Travel advisories issued for Cyprus, Turkey, Egypt following expansion of conflict to British RAF Akrotiri base
Emirates airline cancelled flights to Dubai due to Iranian missile attacks on UAE
Gulf region aviation significantly disrupted — multiple flights cancelled, airspace closures
supply_chain: Saudi Ras Tanura refinery attacked — debris from intercepted drones caused limited fire at one of world's largest oil facilities
Amazon data services disrupted in UAE due to Iranian missile attacks
Ras al-Khaimah in UAE struck by Iranian ballistic missiles — maritime and logistics hub impacted
Port operations in Bahrain (Salman Port) targeted — potential supply chain disruption to 5th Fleet logistics
market_macro: Oil prices surge to nearly $80/barrel (Brent Crude 52-week high) on Strait of Hormuz shipping fears
Oil spike of 7% to $74/barrel reported earlier — significant energy market volatility
UK motoring industry warns of record fuel prices at pumps linked to Iran conflict

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict will continue escalating with sustained Iranian missile waves against Israel and US Gulf bases. Iranian air defense demonstrated capability to target US aircraft, increasing risk to US aviation. Hezbollah formal entry into war expands front to Lebanon. Expect continued strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure and potential further expansion to additional targets. Iran has rejected negotiations, leaving military resolution as primary pathway.
escalation_risks: Nuclear site strike (Natanz) — unverified but would represent massive escalation triggering regional war
Additional US aircraft losses to Iranian air defenses — demonstrated capability increases risk
Houthi formal entry into conflict — currently threatened, would add Yemen front
Gulf state casualties from debris/interceptions — could force states to abandon US neutrality
Closing of Strait of Hormuz — oil chokepoint disruption would trigger global energy crisis
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: No visible de-escalation pathway. Iran has explicitly rejected negotiations (Larijani statement). Trump administration demanding unconditional surrender of weapons. Khamenei's death eliminates key figure for potential negotiations. Regional powers (Gulf states) condemning Iran but not mediating. International community has not proposed ceasefire framework.
